results.title: Proactive AI Agents: Definition, Core Components, and Business ...
results.content:
No. Proactive agents can enhance automations by adding context and initiative, but they provide different benefits. Automations follow defined rules — if x happens, then do y. Proactive agents use machine learning and large language models (LLMs) to learn context from Slack messages and other internal communication and documentation. They use this context to make recommendations or spot issues before problems get out of hand.

No. Proactive agents are collaborative tools designed to support — not replace — human judgment. They assist humans by identifying problems or inefficiencies, then taking steps to address them, escalating to humans as necessary. They learn how to help humans from humans themselves. [...] When used in Slack, where there’s valuable context living in messages, documents, dashboards, and meeting notes, a proactive agent learns quickly and becomes a valuable asset for modern businesses.

## What makes an AI agent proactive?

A proactive AI agent doesn’t wait to be prompted by a human before taking action. Many agents people interact with require a prompt before the tech addresses the need — like customer service chatbots that surface information after someone starts a conversation. Proactive agents can start addressing a need before a human prompts it. [...] -

# Proactive AI Agents: Definition, Core Components, and Business Value

By the team at Slack30th January 2026

11 min read

Proactive agents are a type of agentic AI designed to support human teams without constant prompting. They can help their human counterparts with a range of manual tasks. For example, a proactive agent might analyze patterns in a key account and surface churn risks to sales leaders, or it might identify an IT issue based on a cluster of account bugs before the problem becomes a full-scale outage.

results.title: GTM Engine Slack Integration for Workflow Automation
results.content:
2. AI Revenue Agents in Slack

RevOps teams can build and deploy custom AI agents directly into Slack through GTM Engine's Automations section. These agents have full context of your organization's revenue data, including pipeline, deal progress, forecast, contacts, and account history. They can answer questions, surface risks, and deliver insights on demand. Agents can be named and branded however your team prefers, and any agent can be deployed to a channel or DM.

## How It Works in Slack

Once connected, GTM Engine operates in two modes inside Slack:

Automated Notifications [...] Automated Notifications

Workflows built in GTM Engine's Workflow Builder can include a Slack Message task. When a workflow triggers, whether post-meeting, on a CRM field change, on a risk flag, or on a schedule, it delivers a formatted message to the designated Slack channel or user. Messages can use plain text or Slack Block Kit for richer formatting.

Deployed AI Agents

RevOps users with access to the Automations section of GTM Engine can create custom AI agents, configure their data scope and toolset, and deploy them to Slack. Once deployed, team members can mention the agent in any channel or DM it has been added to and ask revenue-related questions in plain language. The agent responds using live data from your CRM and GTM Engine's intelligence layer.

Documentation media [...] To set up automated notifications:

 Navigate to the Workflows Library
 Open or create a workflow
 Add a Slack Message task to any workflow step
 Select the destination channel (e.g., #sales-team) or individual user
 Choose plain text or Block Kit formatting
 Save and publish your workflow

To deploy an AI agent to Slack:

 Navigate to Automations → Agents (RevOps and Admin roles only)
 Create a new agent or open an existing one
 Configure the agent's name, data scope, and available tools
 Click Deploy to Slack and select the channel or DM
 Team members can immediately begin asking the agent revenue questions

Add to Slack
